description: Top-level bug taxonomy for Qiskit programs, derived from the Bugs4Q benchmark paper.
source: Zhao et al., 'Bugs4Q: A Benchmark of Real Bugs for Quantum Programs', ASE 2021

classes.label: Incorrect Gate
classes.description: The wrong gate type is applied at a given point in the circuit (e.g., X instead of Z, H instead of I).
examples: using X where Z was intended

classes.label: Wrong Qubit Order
classes.description: The correct gate is applied but the qubit arguments are in the wrong order (control/target swap, register mismatch).

classes.label: Missing Measurement
classes.description: The circuit is not measured before execution, or the measurement is applied to the wrong qubits/bits.

classes.label: Circuit Structure
classes.description: The overall topology or ordering of gates in the circuit is incorrect (e.g., missing a layer, extra barrier, wrong depth).

classes.label: Initialisation
classes.description: The QuantumCircuit or registers are initialised incorrectly (wrong qubit count, wrong initial state).

classes.label: Classical Control
classes.description: Classical register size or classical-if conditions are wrong.

classes.label: Runtime / API Error
classes.description: Code that triggers a Qiskit or Python runtime error due to incorrect API usage.

classes.label: Other
classes.description: A bug that does not fit neatly into any of the above categories.

classes.label: Unknown
classes.description: Used when the pipeline cannot determine or has not yet determined the bug class.
